mechanism_narrative: A persistently low FEDFUNDS rate keeps discount rates compressed and supports risk appetite, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days when policy remains pinned near zero and inflation is not reaccelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward Treasury yield changes are positive in most low-rate episodes, or if the sign flips when FEDFUNDS stays near zero and CPIAUCSL is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening labor demand and slower growth expectations, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days as investors price easier policy and weaker real activity.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if yield changes are not negative after unemployment shocks, or if yield declines occur equally when UNRATE is stable and PAYEMS is accelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates stronger final demand and payroll growth, which lifts near-term growth expectations and causes the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the yield does not rise when both retail sales and payrolls accelerate together, or if either series alone explains the effect without the joint signal.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after a prior slump signals improving industrial capacity utilization and stronger real activity, which raises the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days through higher expected growth and term premium.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if post-rebound periods in INDPRO do not show positive 60-day yield drift, or if yield rises occur without any industrial production recovery.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already above 1 percent and CPIAUCSL continues to rise, markets infer policy tightening will persist, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 5 trading days as front-end rates and inflation expectations reprice upward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1.0% and CPIAUCSL inflation is rising on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon yield increases do not cluster after the joint tightening-and-inflation condition, or if either variable alone matches the signal better than the pair.

mechanism_narrative: A weak GDP and retail-sales combination indicates broad demand deceleration, which lowers the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days as investors price slower nominal growth and lower real rates.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is negative or near zero while RSAFS is below its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if weak GDP plus weak retail sales does not precede lower yields on a 60-day horizon, or if the effect disappears once one of the two demand proxies is removed.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market deterioration regime, falling PAYEMS together with rising UNRATE signals a broad employment contraction that depresses the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days as recession odds increase.
failure_condition: Reject if yield declines are not concentrated in episodes where payrolls fall and unemployment rises together, or if the sign reverses during clear labor-market weakening.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts rebound after prior weakness signals stabilization in cyclical construction demand and future inflation-sensitive activity, which lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days when HOUST crosses above its recent trend.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 3-month moving average after a prior 2-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts rebounds are not followed by higher yields on a 20-day horizon, or if the signal fails when inflation is stable to firm.
